what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that points to a board item via a board:// URI.
observations: The file contains only YAML front matter and no body content.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference an external board item.
The file is purely a linkage/provenance stub with no payload.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Filename is att-<uuid>.md matching the id.
References external entities with a URI-style target.
concerns: No body content or context means the attachment's meaning depends entirely on the referenced board item.
The semantics of 'board_items' and the board:// scheme are not defined inside the file.
notes: A minimal channel-attachment stub that links att-ad2426f8 to board item item-e9b44885 in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or state does board item item-e9b44885 represent?
Is the empty body intentional, or should the attachment include a summary or snapshot?
What are the exact semantics of the board_items attachment_type and board:// target scheme?
apparent_themes: Decoupled referencing of external artifacts via persistent identifiers.
Lightweight provenance records in channel memory.
Separation of attachment metadata from attached content.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, addressable association between a channel attachment and a board item without duplicating the board content in the attachment file.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as the target value in attachment metadata is a distinct linking convention.
contribution: Provides a stable, named attachment record (att-ad2426f8) that records when and to which board item it was linked in the dragnet-development channel, preserving provenance for fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel attachment file named att-ad2426f8 in the dragnet-development channel with attachment_type board_items and target board://item-e9b44885, created on 2026-06-23.
intention_for_embedding: To persistently associate a channel attachment with a specific board item for channel memory indexing.
